chapter_title.ar: باب الْأَمْرِ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَ النَّهْيِ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – The Enjoining of the good and the Forbidding of the evil

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/401)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/401)
text: مَا قُدِّسَتْ أُمَّةٌ لَمْ يُؤْخَذْ لِضَعِيفِهَا مِنْ قَوِيِّهَا بِحَقِّهِ غَيْرَ مُتَعْتَعٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from a group of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A community cannot be sanctified, the one which does not take for its weak ones, its rights from its strong ones without stirring’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from a group of our people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A people, who does not take back the rights of the weak ones among them from the stronger ones, without causing any harm, can never become holy.’”
